title: Google Loses Final Appeal Against Record EU Fine
subtitle: Europe's highest court confirms a massive penalty for Android business practices

summary.en: Google has officially lost its final appeal against a multi-billion euro fine from the European Union. The case centers on claims that the tech giant used its Android operating system to unfairly block competitors. European regulators argued that Google forced phone manufacturers to pre-install its own apps. Although Google has already adjusted its agreements to follow the rules, it must now pay the full penalty. This decision marks the end of a long legal battle for the company.
summary.zh: Google 已正式輸掉針對歐盟數十億歐元罰款的最終上訴。此案核心在於指控該科技巨頭利用其 Android 作業系統不公平地封鎖競爭對手。歐洲監管機構主張 Google 強迫手機製造商預先安裝自家應用程式。儘管 Google 已調整協議以遵守規定，但現在仍必須支付全額罰款。這項裁決標誌著該公司一場長期法律戰的終結。

paragraphs.en: Google has reached the end of a long legal battle with European regulators. Europe’s highest court has officially rejected the company’s final appeal, confirming that Google must pay a record-setting fine of €4.1 billion. This penalty was originally issued due to claims that the tech giant used its Android mobile operating system to unfairly block its rivals.
paragraphs.zh: Google 已結束與歐洲監管機構之間長期的法律訴訟。歐洲最高法院正式駁回了該公司的最終上訴，確認 Google 必須支付一筆創紀錄的 41 億歐元罰款。這項處罰最初是因為該科技巨頭被指控利用其 Android 行動作業系統不公平地封鎖競爭對手而開出。

paragraphs.en: According to BBC Business, the European Commission first handed out a fine of €4.3 billion in 2018. A lower court later reduced this amount to €4.1 billion in 2022. With this latest ruling from the Court of Justice of the European Union, Google has no more options to challenge the decision. Ars Technica reports that the company will now have to pay the full amount, which is approximately $4.7 billion.
paragraphs.zh: 根據 BBC 商業新聞報導，歐盟執委會（European Commission）最初於 2018 年開出 43 億歐元的罰單。下級法院隨後於 2022 年將金額減至 41 億歐元。隨著歐盟法院（Court of Justice of the European Union）做出這項最新裁決，Google 已無其他途徑可以挑戰此決定。科技媒體 Ars Technica 報導指出，該公司現在必須支付全額罰款，約合 47 億美元。

paragraphs.en: The case focused on how Google managed its relationships with companies that make Android smartphones and tablets. European antitrust regulators — officials who work to ensure fair competition in the market — argued that Google acted illegally in three main ways. First, the company required manufacturers to pre-install the Google Search app and the Chrome browser as a condition for using the Google Play app store. Second, Google made payments to large manufacturers and mobile network operators to ensure they only used Google Search on their devices. Finally, the company prevented manufacturers from selling devices that used alternative, modified versions of the Android system by threatening to block their access to Google’s apps.
paragraphs.zh: 此案重點在於 Google 如何管理其與 Android 智慧型手機及平板電腦製造商之間的關係。歐洲反壟斷監管機構——即致力於確保市場公平競爭的官員——主張 Google 在三個主要方面有違法行為。首先，該公司要求製造商必須預先安裝 Google 搜尋應用程式與 Chrome 瀏覽器，作為使用 Google Play 應用程式商店的條件。其次，Google 向大型製造商及行動網路營運商支付費用，以確保他們在裝置上僅使用 Google 搜尋。最後，該公司透過威脅封鎖製造商存取 Google 應用程式的權限，阻止他們販售使用替代性、經修改版 Android 系統的裝置。

paragraphs.en: In response to the court's decision, a Google spokesperson stated that the judgment fails to recognize the company's significant investment in keeping Android open and free. The spokesperson also noted that Google had already changed its agreements to follow the initial 2018 ruling. Google’s chief executive, Sundar Pichai, previously argued that the company’s business model actually creates more choice for users rather than less. He noted that the Android system does not stop device owners from downloading other web browsers or using different search engines.
paragraphs.zh: 針對法院的裁決，Google 發言人表示，該判決未能認可公司在保持 Android 開放與免費方面所投入的重大貢獻。發言人也指出，Google 早已修改其協議以遵守 2018 年的初步裁決。Google 執行長皮查伊（Sundar Pichai）先前曾主張，該公司的商業模式實際上為使用者創造了更多而非更少的選擇。他指出，Android 系統並未阻止裝置擁有者下載其他網頁瀏覽器或使用不同的搜尋引擎。

paragraphs.en: This legal defeat is part of a larger trend of European authorities targeting Google’s parent company, Alphabet. The European Commission has taken action against the firm several times in recent years. For example, in September 2024, regulators ruled that Google must pay a €2.4 billion fine for abusing its market power in shopping-comparison services. Furthermore, in September 2025, the company was fined €2.95 billion for favoring its own products in online advertising, which regulators said hurt the business of its rivals.
paragraphs.zh: 這次法律上的挫敗，是歐洲當局針對 Google 母公司 Alphabet 採取一連串行動的趨勢之一。歐盟執委會近年來已多次對該公司採取行動。例如，在 2024 年 9 月，監管機構裁定 Google 因濫用其在購物比價服務中的市場支配地位，必須支付 24 億歐元罰款。此外，在 2025 年 9 月，該公司因在線上廣告中偏袒自家產品而遭罰 29.5 億歐元，監管機構稱此舉損害了競爭對手的業務。

paragraphs.en: Some experts have compared this situation to past legal actions taken against Microsoft. Years ago, the European Union forced Microsoft to offer users a choice of web browsers to reduce the dominance of Internet Explorer. However, Ars Technica noted that by the time those rules were fully put into place, Microsoft’s browser dominance was already fading. In contrast, Google’s market position remains very strong, even though the company introduced similar "ballot screens" on Android devices to give users more choices after the first fine was announced in 2018.
paragraphs.zh: 部分專家將此情況與過去針對微軟（Microsoft）的法律行動相提並論。多年前，歐盟曾強迫微軟提供使用者選擇網頁瀏覽器的機會，以降低 Internet Explorer 的壟斷地位。然而，Ars Technica 指出，當這些規則完全落實時，微軟瀏覽器的市場主導地位早已式微。相比之下，儘管 Google 在 2018 年首次罰款宣布後，已在 Android 裝置上引入類似的「選擇畫面」以提供使用者更多選擇，但其市場地位依然非常穩固。

paragraphs.en: While this €4.1 billion fine is a significant amount, it is not the largest penalty Google has faced globally. In October 2024, a Russian court issued a massive fine against the company for restricting Russian state media channels on YouTube. That specific penalty reached two undecillion roubles, a figure that is higher than the total value of all goods and services produced in the world, known as the global GDP.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然這筆 41 億歐元的罰款金額龐大，但並非 Google 在全球面臨的最大處罰。2024 年 10 月，俄羅斯法院對該公司開出了一筆鉅額罰款，原因是其在 YouTube 上限制了俄羅斯官方媒體頻道。該項處罰金額高達 2 澗（undecillion）盧布，這個數字甚至超過了全球所有商品與服務生產的總價值，即全球國內生產毛額（GDP）。

paragraphs.en: For now, the focus for Google remains on navigating these complex regulatory environments. While the company continues to emphasize its commitment to innovation, the recent court ruling shows that European regulators will continue to watch the tech giant’s business practices closely to ensure a fair marketplace for all.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，Google 的重心仍放在應對這些複雜的監管環境。儘管該公司持續強調其對創新的承諾，但近期的法院裁決顯示，歐洲監管機構將繼續密切關注這家科技巨頭的商業行為，以確保市場對所有人而言皆公平公正。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To prevent someone from doing something or to stop something from happening.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（街區、積木），這裡作動詞用。文中：This penalty was originally issued due to claims that the tech giant used its Android mobile operating system to unfairly block its rivals.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To officially give or impose a punishment or decision.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語在文中指官方給予處罰，而非字面上的「遞送」。文中：According to BBC Business, the European Commission first handed out a fine of €4.3 billion in 2018.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To direct criticism or legal action at a specific person or organization.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（目標），這裡作動詞用，指監管機構採取行動的對象。文中：This legal defeat is part of a larger trend of European authorities targeting Google’s parent company, Alphabet.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To slowly become weaker or less important.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於描述顏色變淡，這裡形容市場影響力減弱。文中：However, Ars Technica noted that by the time those rules were fully put into place, Microsoft’s browser dominance was already fading.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Google must pay €4.1bn fine for using Android to 'block' rivals

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Google loses long-running appeal of record EU fine, will have to cough up $4.7 billion

previousArticles.title: Google Loses Final Appeal Against Record EU Fine
previousArticles.summaryEn: Europe’s highest court has officially rejected Google’s appeal regarding a massive antitrust fine. The company was originally penalized for using its Android operating system to unfairly limit competition. Regulators argued that Google forced manufacturers to pre-install its own apps, such as Search and Chrome. Although Google argued its business model supports innovation, the court has now confirmed the penalty. This decision marks the end of a long legal battle that began in 2018.

quiz.question: According to the article, which of the following was one of the specific practices regulators cited as illegal regarding Google's Android business?

options.A: Forcing all Android users to pay a monthly subscription fee for the Google Play store.
options.B: Preventing manufacturers from selling devices that used modified versions of the Android system.
options.C: Restricting the number of third-party apps that could be downloaded onto a single device.
options.D: Prohibiting mobile network operators from selling smartphones made by companies other than Google.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，監管機構指控 Google 的非法行為之一是「阻止製造商銷售使用替代性、修改版 Android 系統的設備，並威脅將封鎖其使用 Google 應用程式的權限」。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 錯誤，因為 Google 是要求預裝應用程式，而非限制下載數量；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到的是支付款項給製造商以確保其僅使用 Google 搜尋，而非禁止銷售其他品牌手機。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the effectiveness of the European Union's regulatory interventions on Google's market position?

options.A: The fines have successfully caused Google's market dominance to collapse entirely.
options.B: The interventions have forced Google to immediately cease all business operations in Europe.
options.C: Despite regulatory penalties and mandatory changes, Google has maintained a very strong market presence.
options.D: Google's market power has significantly decreased because the 'ballot screens' were highly effective.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第六段，文章將 Google 與微軟進行對比，提到儘管歐盟推動了類似的「選擇畫面（ballot screens）」，但 Google 的市場地位依然「非常強勢（remains very strong）」。選項 A 與文章描述不符；選項 B 過於誇大，文章並未提到 Google 停止營運；選項 D 錯誤，文章暗示這些措施對於削弱 Google 的市場主導地位效果有限，不像當初對微軟那樣顯著。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'navigating' mean in the context of 'navigating these complex regulatory environments'?

options.A: Steering a ship through dangerous waters.
options.B: Successfully avoiding any future legal consequences.
options.C: Dealing with and managing difficult situations.
options.D: Traveling to different countries to meet regulators.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。在文中，「navigating」並非指航海，而是比喻性用法，意指「應對、處理」複雜的監管環境。選項 A 是該詞的字面義，與文意不符；選項 B 太過絕對，因為 Google 仍在面臨監管；選項 D 雖然包含移動的意思，但並非此處表達的商業管理意涵。此處強調的是企業在面對法律挑戰時的應對能力。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: Google has decided to stop using the Android operating system due to high legal costs.
options.B: The European Union is the only region in the world that has ever fined Google for antitrust violations.
options.C: Google is facing a series of legal challenges from European regulators aimed at ensuring fair market competition.
options.D: The Russian court's fine against Google is the most important legal development for the company's future.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。整篇文章的核心在於報導 Google 在歐洲面臨的法律訴訟（特別是 Android 案的最終判決）以及歐盟監管機構長期以來對其市場行為的審查。選項 A 與事實不符；選項 B 錯誤，文章明確提到俄羅斯法院也對 Google 處以鉅額罰款；選項 D 雖有提及俄羅斯的罰款，但那只是作為補充資訊，並非文章探討歐洲監管趨勢的主軸。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
